title: hieracium maculatum subsp. pollichiae
intro: Hieracium maculatum subsp. pollichiae, a lesser-known subspecies of the spotted hawkweed plant, is a perennial herb native to southern Europe, characterized by its yellow daisy-like flowers and spotted leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Hieracium maculatum subsp. pollichiae include reducing inflammation, soothing sore throats, and alleviating cold symptoms. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve infusions or decoctions of its leaves and flowers, which can be consumed as teas or used as topical treatments for skin conditions.
